summary:
Glycopyrrolate is preferred over other medications due to its rapid onset, high potency, and superior effectiveness in managing secretions during surgery. It has gained widespread use based on extensive research supporting its safety and efficacy across different patient groups and procedure types. The conclusion focuses only on two main aspects—onset time and comparative strength—and omits less critical details about individual drug properties not directly related to common usage preferences among clinicians. To maintain brevity while capturing essential elements:

Glycopyrrolate is favored because it acts quickly and strongly against excessive saliva production; numerous studies confirm its broad applicability making it the go-to choice globally for effective preoperative preparation. Key Points: Rapid Onset & High Potency
Widely Used Due to Extensive Research Supporting Safety/Efficacy Across Populations/Procedures Summary ends here. Agent should now proceed accordingly if further action needed post-summary completion step. ```plaintext
Key Point 1: Glycopyrrolate provides fast-acting effects suitable just prior to anesthesia.
Key Point 2: Numerous studies support its broader application compared to alternatives.
```
